description:
## Background

Beadsmith currently keeps the active Issue List View in application state and keeps Issue Search and selected Issue inside Issue Explorer. These independent state owners cannot represent a durable renderer destination, cannot support Back/Forward correctly, and currently clear selection whenever either a view or search hides the selected row. The accepted navigation decision uses Wouter and same-origin routes as the renderer navigation contract.

Triage: `ready-for-agent`.

## What to build

Make the Issue Explorer destination route-owned within one Current Workspace. A user can navigate to `/issues`, select an Issue through a real link, change Issue List Views, edit search, refresh derived data, and see the route, list, selection, detail state, and title remain coherent. A routed ID absent from the authoritative Issue List renders Issue-not-found instead of silently clearing the route.

This slice establishes the route contract and visible behavior. Back/Forward controls, Settings routing, Issue References, and external deep-link delivery belong to later children.

## Technical Approach

- Add Wouter and install a top-level Router with Issue List and Issue Detail route matching.
- Use `/issues/<issue-id>` for a selected Issue and `/issues` for no selection.
- Encode non-default Issue List View and raw Issue Search in `view` and `search` query parameters; omit defaults where practical.
- Parse unknown or malformed view values conservatively to the default Issue List View and normalize the URL with replacement rather than creating a second destination.
- Make route/query state authoritative. Remove the competing local selection/search ownership from Issue Explorer and pass the derived destination into the presentation components.
- Resolve selected Issue data from the successful Workspace’s authoritative All Issues collection rather than only the currently visible rows. This preserves Issue Detail when search hides the row.
- Preserve selection during search replacement. When a deliberate Issue List View change excludes the selected Issue, navigate to the destination view without an Issue selection.
- Convert Issue List rows from buttons to Wouter Links while preserving selected styling, `aria-current`, data attributes needed by acceptance tests, keyboard operation, and row accessibility.
- Render an explicit Issue-not-found state in Issue Detail when the route contains an ID that is absent from All Issues. Keep the route active if a later Beadwork refresh removes the Issue.
- Update the window title for resolved Issue, Issue-not-found, and unselected Issue List destinations.
- Keep Workspace switching atomic through the existing transition gate; route ownership must not bypass or duplicate its generation rules.

## Considerations

- Issue IDs can collide across Workspaces. This slice operates only in the Current Workspace; Workspace identity enters navigation state in later children.
- Ready and Blocked can contain alternate Issue objects with an ID also found in All Issues. The route identity is the ID, while authoritative resolution for durable navigation should use All Issues consistently.
- Search can hide the selected row while Issue Detail remains populated. Selected-row styling is absent when no visible row matches, which is intentional.
- Changing an Issue List View only clears selection if the destination base view excludes the Issue. Re-selecting the current view or editing search must not clear it.
- Do not add browser-history controls or custom navigation indexes in this ticket.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Wouter owns `/issues` and `/issues/<issue-id>` renderer destinations.
- [ ] Issue List View and Issue Search round-trip through `view` and `search` query parameters with safe defaults.
- [ ] Selecting an Issue updates the route and renders its Issue Detail.
- [ ] Issue List rows are accessible links with correct selected styling and current-state semantics.
- [ ] Editing Issue Search preserves the selected Issue and Issue Detail even when its row is filtered out.
- [ ] Changing to an Issue List View that excludes the selected Issue produces an unselected destination.
- [ ] A routed missing Issue renders Issue-not-found in Issue Detail and remains routed across data refresh.
- [ ] Window titles distinguish a resolved Issue, Issue-not-found, and the general Issue List.
- [ ] Existing Workspace loading, switching, and atomic snapshot behavior remains green.

## Test Plan

- Add pure route/query contract tests for defaults, encoding, invalid view values, and selected/unselected destinations.
- Update Issue Explorer tests to assert link semantics, search-preserved selection, view-cleared selection, and Issue-not-found behavior.
- Extend application navigation tests to assert URL, rendered destination, and window title together.
- Run renderer tests, TypeScript build, Ultracite, and existing Workspace transition tests.
title: Make Issue Explorer navigation route-owned
